mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for developing agentic workflows, ideal for users looking to self-host AI applications. It provides a robust framework for building and managing AI-driven workflows, but requires careful resource management and configuration for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add the Dify Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dify Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add langgenius https://charts.langgenius.com

```

> This is the official repository for Dify.

steps.title: Install Dify CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Dify to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> Ensure CRDs are installed before deploying the application.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the Dify application in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install dify langgenius/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> Replace '0.12.0' with the desired version if necessary.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource to demonstrate how to use Dify.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: DifyWorkflow
metadata:
  name: example-workflow
spec:
  workflow:
    steps:
      - name: example-step
        action: example-action
EOF

```

> This creates a simple workflow for testing.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: DifyWorkflow
metadata:
  name: example-workflow
spec:
  workflow:
    steps:
      - name: example-step
        action: example-action
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Dify installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the Dify Custom Resource is ready and functioning as expected.

```bash

kubectl get difyworkflow example-workflow -o yaml

```

> Look for 'status: ready' in the output.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Dify application and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all workflows and data associated with Dify.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This command will remove the Dify release.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your workflows. This process includes draining any active workloads, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get difyworkflow -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, spec: .spec}' > backup.json

helm upgrade dify langgenius/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.1

kubectl get difyworkflow example-workflow -o yaml

```

> Backup existing workflows.
> Replace '0.12.1' with the desired version.
> Verify that the upgrade was successful.
commands: kubectl get difyworkflow -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, spec: .spec}' > backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'DifyWorkflow not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified DifyWorkflow resource does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Custom Resource was created successfully with the correct name.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRDs'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRDs may not have been applied correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-run the command to apply the CRDs: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be a conflict with existing resources or incorrect Helm chart version.

**Fix:**
Check for existing resources and ensure the correct version is specified.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Workflow not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The DifyWorkflow may not have completed initialization.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Dify pods for errors: kubectl logs -l app=dify -n dify

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new workflow actions and improved API stability.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older workflow definitions in favor of the new format.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update existing workflows to conform to the new definition format.

resolution.summary: You should see the DifyWorkflow status as 'ready' when running: kubectl get difyworkflow example-workflow.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: DifyWorkflow
metadata:
  name: example-workflow
spec:
  workflow:
    steps:
      - name: example-step
        action: example-action
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
